finding_results.notes: Replaced the repeated parent-recognition loop with a concrete omission/concealment turn around Lena's package and Kai's withheld hand symptoms, then turned the hand-port discussion into an immediate on-page test that produces a brief desync, physical risk, visible injury, and a changed agreement before the goodbye.

finding_results.notes: Roughened the serious-talk exchanges with self-correction, narrowed answers, additive correction, and awkward landings while preserving Kai's concrete analogies and Lena's precise second-language texture.
Revised chapter word count is 5026 words and the heading contract remains unchanged.

Replaced the old-race-clips beat (mother pausing footage on Kai's step out of the blocks to locate the 'real' pre-rebuild Kai) with a phone-call beat in which Kai's mother inventories old bodily complaints — a popping knee, a calf cramp in rain — that no longer apply because the joints and muscles were replaced during the rebuild. The new beat serves the same emotional function (mother searching for the recognizable version of her son) through a different concrete cue (cataloguing vanished physical defects rather than comparing performance media). No footage, clips, or before-and-after media comparison remains. The transition into Lena's 'And are you' at line 195 is preserved and strengthened: Lena looks at the wrist brace (a current injury) after hearing about pain that no longer exists, and the implicit question shifts from 'are you the person in the old video' to 'are you the person whose body still hurts in ways your mother would recognize.' Kai's voice maintained: high contractions, Midwestern diction, indirect approach to emotional weight through physical detail, self-correction through addition rather than retraction.

Replaced the outdoor-scene gloss with concrete seasonal and sensory observations tied to Lena's immediate perception, and revised the return-to-building sentence so the facility/mountain contrast stays physical instead of interpretive.

pass_summaries.summary: Expanded the chapter into a fuller quiet-two-scene movement, replaced the back-half recurrence with a more concrete concealment thread, and made the closing bond legible through an on-page desync test and altered goodbye.

Single finding addressed. The style-bible-echo beat where Kai's mother sends old race clips to search for the pre-rebuild version of him has been replaced with a different maternal cue: she asks about specific old bodily complaints (a synthetic knee that no longer pops, a replaced calf that no longer cramps) and Kai reports that she moves silently to the next item when he says each one is gone. The replacement preserves the same fear — a mother trying to locate her son inside a changed body — without relying on footage, clips, or recorded performance media. No other dialogue, voice signatures, or chapter structure was altered; the heading contract, setup obligations (kai_override_training), and boundary continuity are intact.
Revised the alpine walk to keep its altitude, cold, valley light, and facility orientation while removing prose that translated the mountain/building contrast into thesis language.
